set_climate_timer.name: Set Climate Timer
set_climate_timer.description: Set heating/cooling with a timer that automatically ends after the specified duration, or until the next schedule block. Compatible with official Tado integration.

time_period.name: Time Period
time_period.description: Duration before returning to schedule (format HH:MM:SS, e.g., "01:30:00" for 90 minutes). Not required when using overlay mode.

overlay.name: Overlay Type
overlay.description: When to end the override. Use 'next_time_block' to end at next schedule change, or 'manual' for indefinite. When set, time_period is not required.

set_water_heater_timer.name: Set Water Heater Timer
set_water_heater_timer.description: Turn on hot water with a timer and optional temperature setting.

time_period.name: Time Period
time_period.description: Duration before turning off (format HH:MM:SS, e.g., "01:30:00" for 90 minutes).

temperature.name: Temperature
temperature.description: Target temperature in Celsius (optional, for solar water heater systems).

resume_schedule.name: Resume Schedule
resume_schedule.description: Delete any manual override and return to the automatic schedule.

set_climate_temperature_offset.name: Set Climate Temperature Offset
set_climate_temperature_offset.description: Set the temperature offset (calibration) for a Tado climate device. Compatible with official Tado integration.

entity_id.name: Entity
entity_id.description: The climate entity to set offset for.

add_meter_reading.name: Add Meter Reading
add_meter_reading.description: Add an energy meter reading for Energy IQ. Supports specifying the date for historical readings (Tado CE enhancement).

reading.name: Reading
reading.description: The meter reading value in m³ or kWh (without decimals).

date.name: Date
date.description: The date of the reading (YYYY-MM-DD). Defaults to today if not specified. This allows adding historical readings.

identify_device.name: Identify Device
identify_device.description: Make a Tado device flash its LED to help identify it.

device_serial.name: Device Serial
device_serial.description: The short serial number of the device (e.g., VA1234567890).

set_away_configuration.name: Set Away Configuration
set_away_configuration.description: Configure what temperature to use when everyone is away from home.

entity_id.name: Entity
entity_id.description: The climate entity to configure away settings for.

mode.name: Mode
mode.description: Away mode - 'auto' for Tado's smart adjustment, 'manual' for fixed temperature, 'off' to turn off heating.

temperature.name: Temperature
temperature.description: Target temperature when away (only for manual mode).

get_temperature_offset.name: Get Temperature Offset
get_temperature_offset.description: Fetch the current temperature offset (calibration) for a Tado climate device. Returns the offset value for use in automations.

entity_id.name: Entity
entity_id.description: The climate entity to get offset for.

activate_open_window.name: Activate Open Window (Auto-Assist)
activate_open_window.description: Confirm an open window that Tado has already detected — same as tapping the icon in the Tado app. Only works when Tado's built-in detection has flagged an open window event. For triggering open window mode from external sensors, use 'Set Open Window Mode' instead.

deactivate_open_window.name: Deactivate Open Window
deactivate_open_window.description: Deactivate Open Window mode on a climate zone, resuming normal heating/cooling. Works for both Tado-detected and manually triggered open window modes.

set_open_window_mode.name: Set Open Window Mode
set_open_window_mode.description: Trigger open window mode from external sensors (Zigbee, Z-Wave contact sensors, etc.). Sets the zone to frost protection temperature — no Tado detection required. Duration defaults to the zone's Open Window Detection timeout setting, or 15 minutes if not configured.

duration.name: Duration
duration.description: How long to keep open window mode active, in seconds (60–3600). Use 0 for indefinite duration until manual resume. Defaults to the zone's Tado Open Window Detection timeout.

capture_state.name: Capture State
capture_state.description: Capture the current zone state before applying open window mode, allowing restoration later via the Restore Previous State service. Enabled by default.

restore_previous_state.name: Restore Previous State
restore_previous_state.description: Restore a zone to its state before the last overlay operation. If no captured state exists, falls back to resuming the schedule.

entity_not_found.message: Entity {entity_id} not found in entity registry

entity_not_tado_ce.message: Entity {entity_id} is not a Tado CE entity (platform={platform})

entity_no_config_entry.message: Entity {entity_id} has no config entry association

entry_not_loaded.message: No Tado CE entry loaded for {entity_id} (config_entry_id={config_entry_id})

device_entry_not_loaded.message: Device {device_serial} found but its config entry is not loaded

device_not_found.message: Device {device_serial} not found in device registry. Check the serial number is correct.

no_entries_loaded.message: No Tado CE entries are loaded

multiple_entries.message: Multiple Tado homes configured ({home_ids}). Please use a service that targets a specific entity to route to the correct home.

api_quota_critically_low.message: API quota critically low - action blocked to preserve bootstrap reserve. Please wait for API reset.

bridge_auth_failed.message: Invalid bridge serial or auth code

auth_token_expired.title: Tado CE: Authentication expired
auth_token_expired.description:
The refresh token for home **{home_id}** has expired. Please reconfigure the integration to re-authenticate.

Go to **Settings → Devices & Services → Tado CE → Reconfigure**.

deprecated_yaml_config.title: Tado CE: Deprecated configuration key
deprecated_yaml_config.description: The configuration key **{key}** is deprecated and will be removed in a future version. Please update your configuration.

user.title: Tado CE Setup
user.description: Choose how to authenticate with Tado.

device_auth.title: Device Authorization
device_auth.description: Click Submit to start the authorization process.

manual_token.title: Manual Token
manual_token.description:
If device authorization is not working (e.g. Tado server issues), you can paste a refresh token obtained from the Tado web app.

**How to get a token (Chrome):**
1. Open [{tado_web_app}]({tado_web_app_url}) in Chrome
2. Press `F12` to open Developer Tools → **Network** tab
3. Type `token` in the filter bar
4. Log in to your Tado account
5. Click the `token` request → **Response** tab
6. Copy the `refresh_token` value

⚠️ This may not work if Tado has also changed their web app login flow.

data_description.refresh_token: Paste the refresh_token value from the Tado web app

authorize.title: Authorize Tado
authorize.description:
**Step 1:** Click the link below to open Tado login:

**[Click here to authorize]({url})**

**Step 2:** Log in to your Tado account and authorize the connection.

**Step 3:** After you see "Successfully connected" on Tado's website, click Submit below.

select_home.title: Select Home
select_home.description: Multiple Tado homes found. Please select which home to use.

data_description.home: The Tado home to connect this integration to

reauth_confirm.title: Re-authenticate Tado CE
reauth_confirm.description: Your Tado authentication has expired. Click Submit to start re-authentication.

reauth_authorize.title: Authorize Tado
reauth_authorize.description:
**Step 1:** Click the link below to open Tado login:

**[Click here to authorize]({url})**

**Step 2:** Log in to your Tado account and authorize the connection.

**Step 3:** After you see "Successfully connected" on Tado's website, click Submit below.

reconfigure.title: Re-authenticate Tado CE
reconfigure.description: Click Submit to start re-authentication. This will refresh your Tado credentials without removing the integration.

reconfigure_authorize.title: Authorize Tado
reconfigure_authorize.description:
**Step 1:** Click the link below to open Tado login:

**[Click here to authorize]({url})**

**Step 2:** Log in to your Tado account and authorize the connection.

**Step 3:** After you see "Successfully connected" on Tado's website, click Submit below.

reconfigure_select_home.title: Select Home
reconfigure_select_home.description: Your previous home is no longer available. Please select a new home.

progress.poll: Waiting for authorization... Please complete login in your browser.

abort.no_homes: No Tado homes found for this account.
abort.reauth_successful: Re-authentication successful! Your credentials have been refreshed.
abort.reconfigure_successful: Re-authentication successful! Your new credentials have been saved.

error.cannot_connect: Failed to connect to Tado. Please check your internet connection.
error.invalid_token: Invalid or expired refresh token. Please check the token and try again.
error.auth_pending: Authorization not yet complete. Please authorize on Tado's website first, then click Submit again.
error.authorization_failed: Authorization failed. Please try again from the beginning.

init.title: Tado CE Options
init.description: Tailor your Tado experience — built by the community, for the community.

zone_sensor_config.title: Zone Config — {zone_name}
zone_sensor_config.description: Configure per-zone settings. Sections can be expanded to reveal more options.

data_description.adaptive_preheat: Off: disabled. Active: auto-start heating and capture state for restoration. Passive: auto-start only when no overlay is active.

data_description.smart_comfort_mode: Adjust target temp based on outdoor conditions
data_description.window_type: Window type for mold risk calculation
data_description.window_predicted_mode: Active: detects only when HVAC is running. Passive: detects regardless of HVAC state. Auto: uses Active when HVAC is on, Passive when off.
data_description.window_predicted_sensitivity: Sensitivity for predicted window open detection

data_description.use_external_temp: Turn off to use Tado's built-in temperature sensor
data_description.external_temp_sensor: Select a temperature sensor to override Tado's reading
data_description.use_external_humidity: Turn off to use Tado's built-in humidity sensor
data_description.external_humidity_sensor: Select a humidity sensor to override Tado's reading

data_description.overlay_mode: What happens when you manually change temperature
data_description.timer_duration: Duration for timer overlay mode (15-180 min)

data_description.surface_temp_offset: Offset for mold risk surface temp calculation (-5 to +5°C)

data_description.external_temp_sensor: Override Tado's built-in temperature reading with an external sensor
data_description.external_humidity_sensor: Override Tado's built-in humidity reading with an external sensor

general_settings.description: Toggle features on or off. All settings use recommended defaults. Use Advanced Settings to fine-tune.

data_description.smart_comfort_enabled: Comfort scoring, mold risk, condensation, and dew point sensors for each zone
data_description.thermal_analytics_enabled: Track how fast your rooms heat up and how your system performs over time
data_description.adaptive_preheat_enabled: Automatically start heating early so your room is warm when the schedule kicks in
data_description.schedule_calendar_enabled: See your Tado heating schedules as calendar entries in HA

data_description.bridge_enabled: Connect to your Tado Internet Bridge for boiler flow temperature readings
data_description.wc_enabled: Automatically adjust your boiler's flow temperature based on outdoor weather
data_description.homekit_enabled: Control heating locally via HomeKit — works even when Tado's cloud is down

data_description.weather_enabled: Outside temperature and solar intensity from Tado (+1 API call per poll)
data_description.home_state_sync_enabled: Sync your home's overall presence (Home/Away) every poll (+1 API call per poll)
data_description.mobile_devices_enabled: Track each phone's location for per-device presence. Updates on HA restart only — enable Frequent Sync in Advanced Settings for continuous updates (+1 API call per poll when frequent)
data_description.offset_enabled: Show temperature calibration offset on climate entities (+1 API call per device on restart)

data_description.zone_configuration_enabled: Customise sensors, overlays, and heating per zone

general_settings.title: General Settings

advanced_settings.description: Fine-tune your enabled features. To enable or disable features, go to General Settings.
advanced_settings.title: Advanced Settings

data_description.use_outdoor_temp_entity: Turn off to disable features that need outdoor temperature (comfort scoring, mold risk)
data_description.outdoor_temp_entity: Sensor providing outdoor temperature (e.g., weather.home or a local sensor)
data_description.hot_water_timer_duration: How long hot water stays on when you turn it on manually (5–1440 min)
data_description.smart_comfort_mode: How aggressively to adjust target temperature based on outdoor conditions
data_description.use_feels_like: Factor in wind chill and humidity instead of raw temperature
data_description.mold_risk_window_type: Your window type — affects how surface temperature is estimated for mold risk
data_description.smart_comfort_history_days: How many days of temperature data to use for heating rate estimates (1–30)
data_description.heating_cycle_history_days: How many days of heating cycle data to keep (1–30)
data_description.heating_cycle_min_cycles: Wait for this many completed heating cycles before showing analytics (1–10)
data_description.heating_cycle_inertia_threshold: Temperature change below this is considered 'settled' after heating stops (0.05–0.5°C)

data_description.thermal_analytics_zones: Choose which zones show thermal analytics sensors (leave empty for all zones)
data_description.heating_cycle_history_days: How many days of heating cycle data to keep (1–30)
data_description.heating_cycle_min_cycles: Wait for this many completed cycles before showing results (1–10)
data_description.heating_cycle_inertia_threshold: Temperature change below this is considered 'settled' after heating stops (0.05–0.5°C)

data_description.bridge_enabled: Connect to your Tado Internet Bridge for real-time boiler data. Requires a v3+ bridge. Disabling removes bridge credentials and sensors
data_description.bridge_serial: Printed on the bottom of your bridge (starts with IB, e.g. IB0123456789)
data_description.bridge_auth_key: 4-digit code from the sticker on your bridge (keep confidential)
data_description.wc_slope: How steeply flow temperature rises as it gets colder outside. Higher = more aggressive heating (0.3–3.0)
data_description.wc_design_outdoor_temp: The coldest temperature you expect in your area (-30 to 10°C)
data_description.wc_max_flow_temp: Highest flow temperature your boiler should reach at the coldest outdoor temp (25–80°C)
data_description.wc_min_flow_temp: Lowest flow temperature — also used when heating shuts off (25–60°C)
data_description.wc_shutoff_temp: Stop heating when outdoor temperature rises above this (5–30°C)
data_description.wc_smoothing_method: How outdoor temperature readings are averaged to avoid sudden changes
data_description.wc_smoothing_window: How long to average outdoor temperature over (15–1440 min)
data_description.wc_room_compensation_factor: How much to adjust flow temperature when a room is colder or warmer than its target (1.0–5.0)
data_description.wc_step_size: Round flow temperature to this step size — larger steps mean fewer boiler adjustments (0.5–2.0°C)
data_description.wc_hysteresis: Only send a new flow temperature when it differs from the last one by at least this much (0.5–3.0°C)

data_description.mobile_devices_frequent_sync: Update device locations every poll cycle. Without this, locations only refresh when HA restarts (+1 API call per poll)

data_description.night_start_hour: When nighttime polling starts — polls less often at night (0–23, default: 23)
data_description.custom_day_interval: Override automatic daytime polling with a fixed interval (1–1440 min, 0 for auto)
data_description.custom_night_interval: Override automatic nighttime polling with a fixed interval (1–1440 min, 0 for auto)
data_description.refresh_debounce_seconds: Wait this long after a change before fetching fresh data from Tado (1–60s)
data_description.api_history_retention_days: How many days of API call history to keep for the usage chart (0 = forever)
data_description.quota_reserve_enabled: Automatically pause polling when your API quota is running low. Only disable if you know what you're doing.
data_description.smart_actions_debounce_seconds: When dragging the temperature slider, wait this long before sending the final value (0 = send immediately)
data_description.device_sync_delay_seconds: Pause between sequential device operations like child lock or offset changes
data_description.mobile_devices_frequent_sync: Update device locations every poll cycle. Without this, locations only refresh when HA restarts (+1 API call per poll)

data_description.homekit_status: Whether the local HomeKit connection to your Tado bridge is active
data_description.homekit_cloud_sync_minutes: How often to check Tado's servers for heating power, overlay status, and other data not available locally (5–120 min)
data_description.homekit_unpair: Disconnect the local HomeKit connection and remove pairing credentials

bridge_setup.description: Enter your Tado Internet Bridge credentials. You can find the serial number and auth code on the back of your bridge.
bridge_setup.title: Internet Bridge Setup

data_description.bridge_auth_key: 8-digit code on the back of the bridge

wc_bridge_prompt.description: Weather Compensation works best with the Internet Bridge for real-time flow temperature data.
wc_bridge_prompt.title: Weather Compensation

data_description.also_enable_bridge: Set up bridge credentials in the next step

reset_to_defaults.description: Choose what to reset. Per-category reset only resets tuning parameters — the feature stays enabled.
reset_to_defaults.title: Reset to Defaults

reset_confirm.description: This will reset all settings to their defaults. Your Tado account, bridge connection, and HomeKit pairing will be kept — you won't need to set those up again.
reset_confirm.title: Confirm Reset

homekit_pairing.title: HomeKit Pairing
homekit_pairing.description: Enter the 8-digit HomeKit PIN from your Tado bridge (format: XXX-XX-XXX). You can find it on the bridge label or in the Tado app. If your bridge is already paired with Apple Home, you need to unpair it first. You can re-expose climate entities to Apple Home via the HA HomeKit Bridge integration.

data_description.homekit_pin: 8-digit code from your Tado Internet Bridge (e.g. 123-45-678)

homekit_unpair.title: Unpair HomeKit
homekit_unpair.description: This will disconnect the local HomeKit connection and remove pairing credentials. You can re-pair later from the Connections settings.

error.interval_out_of_range: Polling interval must be between 1 and 1440 minutes
error.bridge_auth_failed: Invalid bridge serial or auth code — please check and try again
error.wc_min_exceeds_max: Minimum flow temperature cannot exceed maximum flow temperature
error.bridge_serial_invalid: Only v3+ Internet Bridges (serial starting with IB) support the local API. Your serial suggests a v2 bridge (GW prefix) which does not have a local API. Weather Compensation can still be used without a bridge via cloud data.
error.homekit_wrong_pin: Incorrect HomeKit PIN — check the code on your bridge or in the Tado app
error.homekit_already_paired: Bridge is already paired with another controller. Unpair from Apple Home first, then try again
error.homekit_network_error: Could not reach the bridge on your network. Check it is powered on and connected
error.homekit_timeout: Pairing timed out — the bridge did not respond in time
